meta.title: Address Before the Young Men's Lyceum of Springfield, Illinois

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a written record or account-book entry
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Historical situation is understood through written/accounting records.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Events under the sun are not literally a ledger, but the sentence frames history through accounting.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: historical time and national circumstance are represented as a ledger or journal entry
lexical_units.review_notes: Opening accounting image; useful background but not direct sacrifice evidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a written record or account-book entry
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Historical situation is understood through written/accounting records.

lexical_units.review_notes: Opening accounting image; useful background but not direct sacrifice evidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: ascending onto a physical theatrical stage or platform
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political-historical life is understood through theatrical staging.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Collective historical existence is explicitly framed as appearing on a stage.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: entering historical life as public actors
lexical_units.review_notes: Phrase works as a conventional but traceable stage-of-existence metaphor.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: ascending onto a physical theatrical stage or platform
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political-historical life is understood through theatrical staging.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Collective historical existence is explicitly framed as appearing on a stage.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: entering historical life as public actors
lexical_units.review_notes: Phrase works as a conventional but traceable stage-of-existence metaphor.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: ascending onto a physical theatrical stage or platform
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political-historical life is understood through theatrical staging.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Collective historical existence is explicitly framed as appearing on a stage.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: entering historical life as public actors
lexical_units.review_notes: Phrase works as a conventional but traceable stage-of-existence metaphor.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: raise or construct a physical building
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Institutions are understood through built structure.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Political institutions are explicitly construed as a built edifice.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: building political institutions of liberty and rights
lexical_units.review_notes: Explicit construction metaphor for political institutions.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: raise or construct a physical building
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Institutions are understood through built structure.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Political institutions are explicitly construed as a built edifice.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: building political institutions of liberty and rights
lexical_units.review_notes: Explicit construction metaphor for political institutions.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: move an object onward; keep a material thing from profanation, decay, or tearing
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political inheritance is understood through material preservation and transfer.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Abstract political inheritance is treated as a material object that can be passed, decayed, torn, or profaned.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: preserving and handing on political liberties and institutions
lexical_units.review_notes: Part of the inheritance/building sequence in the Lyceum opening.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: move an object onward; keep a material thing from profanation, decay, or tearing
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political inheritance is understood through material preservation and transfer.

lexical_units.contextual_meaning: preserving and handing on political liberties and institutions
lexical_units.review_notes: Part of the inheritance/building sequence in the Lyceum opening.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: move an object onward; keep a material thing from profanation, decay, or tearing
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political inheritance is understood through material preservation and transfer.

lexical_units.review_notes: Part of the inheritance/building sequence in the Lyceum opening.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a huge human-like being stepping, crushing, or striking
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Geopolitical threat is understood through bodily combat with a giant.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: An overseas army is personified as a giant capable of bodily attack.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: foreign military danger imagined as a giant body attacking physically
lexical_units.review_notes: Direct personifying comparison in the foreign-threat thought experiment.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a huge human-like being stepping, crushing, or striking
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Geopolitical threat is understood through bodily combat with a giant.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: An overseas army is personified as a giant capable of bodily attack.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: foreign military danger imagined as a giant body attacking physically
lexical_units.review_notes: Direct personifying comparison in the foreign-threat thought experiment.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a huge human-like being stepping, crushing, or striking

lexical_units.contextual_meaning: foreign military danger imagined as a giant body attacking physically

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a huge human-like being stepping, crushing, or striking
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Geopolitical threat is understood through bodily combat with a giant.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: An overseas army is personified as a giant capable of bodily attack.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: foreign military danger imagined as a giant body attacking physically
lexical_units.review_notes: Direct personifying comparison in the foreign-threat thought experiment.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a plant or object comes up from the ground
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political emergence is understood through upward growth.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Abstract danger or political actors are represented as emerging upward like growth.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: danger, disorder, or ambitious men arise within the polity
lexical_units.review_notes: Conventional emergence metaphor; retain with moderate confidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: one who writes or completes a work
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Collective causation is understood through making or authoring a work.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Political destruction is framed through authorship and completion.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the nation would cause and complete its own destruction
lexical_units.review_notes: Works with the adjacent suicide formulation.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: one who writes or completes a work
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Collective causation is understood through making or authoring a work.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Political destruction is framed through authorship and completion.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the nation would cause and complete its own destruction
lexical_units.review_notes: Works with the adjacent suicide formulation.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a living organism remains alive
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political existence is understood through bodily or organismic life.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A nation is not a biological organism, but it is construed as capable of living.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the nation continues in political existence
lexical_units.review_notes: Early Lincoln body-politic survival language; compare with Gettysburg nation/live.
Pilot sample reviewed; no MIPVU metaphor-related contrast assigned for this lexical unit.

Pilot sample reviewed; no MIPVU metaphor-related contrast assigned for this lexical unit.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a living organism dies
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political destruction is understood through bodily death.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A nation is not a biological organism, but its political destruction is construed as death.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the nation ceases to exist politically
lexical_units.review_notes: Part of live/die contrast that frames national survival as organismic life.
Pilot sample reviewed; no MIPVU metaphor-related contrast assigned for this lexical unit.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a person intentionally kills himself or herself
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Collective political collapse is understood through self-inflicted bodily death.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A nation cannot literally commit bodily self-killing, but Lincoln construes national destruction through personal suicide.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: self-caused national political destruction
lexical_units.review_notes: High-salience body-politic/personification unit for early Lincoln; candidate support for enemy/internal threat as death-bearing self-destruction.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: human or animal states of fury, wildness, or sobriety
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political emotion is understood through embodied agency.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Passions and judgment are abstract faculties but are treated as agents with temperaments.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: public passions are characterized as uncontrolled agents rather than judgments
lexical_units.review_notes: Useful for the disorder/reason contrast, not direct sacrifice evidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: human or animal states of fury, wildness, or sobriety

lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Passions and judgment are abstract faculties but are treated as agents with temperaments.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: public passions are characterized as uncontrolled agents rather than judgments

lexical_units.basic_meaning: human or animal states of fury, wildness, or sobriety

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a living created being
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Social disorder is understood through animate creation.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A social phenomenon is treated as a creature with an origin.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: mob violence is not produced by climate
lexical_units.review_notes: Marks personification of mob violence as an entity.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a plant or object comes up from the ground
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political emergence is understood through upward growth.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Abstract danger or political actors are represented as emerging upward like growth.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: danger, disorder, or ambitious men arise within the polity
lexical_units.review_notes: Conventional emergence metaphor; retain with moderate confidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: cloth hangings or an object competing with another
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Mob killing is understood through visual covering/ornament.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The sentence explicitly compares corpses on trees with moss or drapery.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: hanged bodies are compared with moss-like drapery in the forest
lexical_units.review_notes: Graphic simile-like image; treat carefully because the dead bodies are literal.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: cloth hangings or an object competing with another
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Mob killing is understood through visual covering/ornament.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The sentence explicitly compares corpses on trees with moss or drapery.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: hanged bodies are compared with moss-like drapery in the forest
lexical_units.review_notes: Graphic simile-like image; treat carefully because the dead bodies are literal.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: offered or killed as a religious sacrifice
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Mob killing is understood through sacrifice.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The killing is historical violence, not a ritual offering, but is named through sacrificial vocabulary.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: a person killed by mob violence is framed as a sacrificial victim
lexical_units.review_notes: Directly relevant to sacrifice vocabulary; keep distinct from endorsed sacrificial ideology.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: being swept off a physical stage or platform
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Life is understood through theatrical staging.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Human death or removal is framed through theatrical stage imagery.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: people are imagined as removed from the arena of life
lexical_units.review_notes: Conventional but explicit stage-of-existence language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: being swept off a physical stage or platform
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Life is understood through theatrical staging.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Human death or removal is framed through theatrical stage imagery.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: people are imagined as removed from the arena of life
lexical_units.review_notes: Conventional but explicit stage-of-existence language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: being swept off a physical stage or platform
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Life is understood through theatrical staging.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Human death or removal is framed through theatrical stage imagery.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: people are imagined as removed from the arena of life
lexical_units.review_notes: Conventional but explicit stage-of-existence language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: bodily faces, falling bodies, victims of physical devastation
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Civic injury is understood through bodily attack.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Political-legal harm is represented through bodily orientation, falling, and ravaging.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: law-abiding people are overcome by mob violence
lexical_units.review_notes: Mob-law violence is partly literal nearby; retain moderate confidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical walls built and walked down
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Law is understood through fortification.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Legal safeguards are not literal walls but are construed as built defenses.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: legal protections are represented as walls defending persons and property
lexical_units.review_notes: Strong legal-fortification metaphor in the mob-law sequence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical walls built and walked down
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Law is understood through fortification.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Legal safeguards are not literal walls but are construed as built defenses.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: legal protections are represented as walls defending persons and property
lexical_units.review_notes: Strong legal-fortification metaphor in the mob-law sequence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical walls built and walked down
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Law is understood through fortification.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: poison, ruin, or physical destruction
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political institutions are understood through harmful/destructible bodies or objects.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Government is abstract-institutional but construed as an entity that can be bane or be annihilated.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: government is treated as a harmful or destructible entity
lexical_units.review_notes: Useful for anti-government affect; not direct sacrifice evidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: poison, ruin, or physical destruction
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political institutions are understood through harmful/destructible bodies or objects.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Government is abstract-institutional but construed as an entity that can be bane or be annihilated.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: government is treated as a harmful or destructible entity
lexical_units.review_notes: Useful for anti-government affect; not direct sacrifice evidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: bodily blood, stepping on a substance, or physically tearing a written charter
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Civic obligation is understood through bodily sacrifice and material violation.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Revolutionary inheritance and legal liberty are treated as bodily/material things that can be spilled, trampled, or torn.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: patriotic obligation and violation of law are framed through blood, trampling, and tearing
lexical_units.review_notes: Historical-semantics note requires control for blood and sacrifice language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: bodily blood, stepping on a substance, or physically tearing a written charter
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Civic obligation is understood through bodily sacrifice and material violation.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Revolutionary inheritance and legal liberty are treated as bodily/material things that can be spilled, trampled, or torn.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: patriotic obligation and violation of law are framed through blood, trampling, and tearing
lexical_units.review_notes: Historical-semantics note requires control for blood and sacrifice language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a living organism continues alive, dies, perishes, survives, or endures bodily strain
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political survival is understood through organismic life and death.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A polity is not a biological organism, but its political existence is construed as bodily life or death.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the nation or government continues or ceases political existence
lexical_units.review_notes: Body-politic survival language; use as MIPVU evidence only with its local national subject.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: breathing, religion, altars, and sacrifice in religious practice
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Civic obedience is understood through religion and sacrifice.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Legal-political reverence is explicitly understood through religious ritual vocabulary.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: reverence for law is framed as religious devotion and ritual sacrifice
lexical_units.review_notes: Central Lyceum political-religion passage; strong but normatively complex sacrifice evidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical props supporting a structure and crumbling through decay
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political stability is understood through built structure.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Institutional maintenance is framed through structural support and material decay.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: early supports for government are represented as structural props that decay
lexical_units.review_notes: Construction metaphor leading into later temple/fabric language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical props supporting a structure and crumbling through decay
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political stability is understood through built structure.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Institutional maintenance is framed through structural support and material decay.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: early supports for government are represented as structural props that decay
lexical_units.review_notes: Construction metaphor leading into later temple/fabric language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: an experiment, proof, or proposition tested for truth
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political viability is understood through experiment and demonstration.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A political order is not a laboratory object, but its success is framed as a trial or proof.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: self-government is treated as a trial whose truth can be demonstrated
lexical_units.review_notes: Term has period republican-rhetoric control; do not reduce to modern laboratory science.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: an experiment, proof, or proposition tested for truth
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political viability is understood through experiment and demonstration.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A political order is not a laboratory object, but its success is framed as a trial or proof.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: self-government is treated as a trial whose truth can be demonstrated
lexical_units.review_notes: Term has period republican-rhetoric control; do not reduce to modern laboratory science.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physically fastening, staking, or linking objects
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Commitment is understood through physical fastening.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Abstract destiny and ambition are represented as attached to an undertaking.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: founders' destiny is bound to the political experiment
lexical_units.review_notes: Adjacent to the experiment/proof sequence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physically fastening, staking, or linking objects
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Commitment is understood through physical fastening.

lexical_units.contextual_meaning: founders' destiny is bound to the political experiment

lexical_units.basic_meaning: literal immortality, death, sinking downward, or forgetting
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Historical memory is understood through life/death and vertical motion.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Reputation and memory are treated through bodily life/death or physical descent.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: fame or memory persists or disappears as life, deathlessness, or downward sinking
lexical_units.review_notes: Memory/fame metaphor, not direct support for sacrifice unless linked to death language.

lexical_units.contextual_meaning: fame or memory persists or disappears as life, deathlessness, or downward sinking
lexical_units.review_notes: Memory/fame metaphor, not direct support for sacrifice unless linked to death language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: literal immortality, death, sinking downward, or forgetting
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Historical memory is understood through life/death and vertical motion.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Reputation and memory are treated through bodily life/death or physical descent.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: fame or memory persists or disappears as life, deathlessness, or downward sinking
lexical_units.review_notes: Memory/fame metaphor, not direct support for sacrifice unless linked to death language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: an experiment, proof, or proposition tested for truth
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political viability is understood through experiment and demonstration.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A political order is not a laboratory object, but its success is framed as a trial or proof.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: self-government is treated as a trial whose truth can be demonstrated
lexical_units.review_notes: Term has period republican-rhetoric control; do not reduce to modern laboratory science.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: an experiment, proof, or proposition tested for truth

lexical_units.contextual_meaning: self-government is treated as a trial whose truth can be demonstrated
lexical_units.review_notes: Term has period republican-rhetoric control; do not reduce to modern laboratory science.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: literal immortality, death, sinking downward, or forgetting

lexical_units.contextual_meaning: fame or memory persists or disappears as life, deathlessness, or downward sinking
lexical_units.review_notes: Memory/fame metaphor, not direct support for sacrifice unless linked to death language.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: game caught in a chase; crops harvested from a field by reapers
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political ambition is understood through hunting and harvest.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Ambition and fame are not literal game or crops, but are framed through hunting and agriculture.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: political glory and ambition are represented as hunting or harvest
lexical_units.review_notes: Extended ambition sequence; keep as rhetorical-metaphor evidence rather than sacrifice evidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a plant or object comes up from the ground
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political emergence is understood through upward growth.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Abstract danger or political actors are represented as emerging upward like growth.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: danger, disorder, or ambitious men arise within the polity
lexical_units.review_notes: Conventional emergence metaphor; retain with moderate confidence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical building, erected structures, or pulling a structure down
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political institutions are understood through built structure.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Political work is construed as construction or demolition of a building.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: political inheritance or institutional work is represented as building and demolition
lexical_units.review_notes: Part of the ambition-versus-institution sequence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical building, erected structures, or pulling a structure down
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political institutions are understood through built structure.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Political work is construed as construction or demolition of a building.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: political inheritance or institutional work is represented as building and demolition
lexical_units.review_notes: Part of the ambition-versus-institution sequence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: animal families/tribes, towers, roads, thirst, burning, or bodily extension
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political ambition is understood through embodied force and animal/combat imagery.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Abstract ambition is repeatedly construed through embodied motion, animal rank, appetite, and fire.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: ambition and genius are represented through animals, height, paths, bodily appetite, fire, or stretching
lexical_units.review_notes: Dense ambition passage; individual labels are conventional but locally clustered.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical smothering, sleep/dormancy, agency, fading light, or dimness
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Collective memory and emotion are understood through embodied or visual states.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Abstract passions and memories are treated as bodies, agents, or lights.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: emotion, memory, or influence loses force or becomes inactive
lexical_units.review_notes: Revolutionary-memory decline sequence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a living person, a written history, testimony borne by a witness, or reading a text
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Collective memory is understood through living bodies and written testimony.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Historical memory is embodied in persons and wounds as if it were a living readable document.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: Revolutionary veterans and family memory are treated as living/readable history
lexical_units.review_notes: Reliability-sample sentence; the wounds are literal, but history/testimony framing is figurative.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a military fortress, artillery, and walls leveled by attack
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Historical memory is understood through military fortification and attack.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Memory is not a fortification, and time does not literally fire artillery.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: living revolutionary memory is represented as a fortress destroyed by time's artillery
lexical_units.review_notes: Extended direct metaphor for the loss of Revolutionary memory.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: trees, hurricanes, trunks, foliage, limbs, storms, and sinking
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Historical generations are understood through trees and weather.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Human generations and memory are explicitly construed as trees weathered by storms.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the Revolutionary generation is represented as a damaged forest of trees
lexical_units.review_notes: Extended tree/weather metaphor following the fortress image.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: temple architecture, pillars, crumbling, falling, hewing stone from a quarry
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Liberty is understood through sacred architecture.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Political liberty and rational support are explicitly construed as architectural materials.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: liberty and institutions are represented as a temple supported by pillars of reason
lexical_units.review_notes: High-salience temple-of-liberty construction metaphor.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical enemies, moldable materials, bodily sleep, trumpets, and waking

lexical_units.basic_meaning: fabric, rock foundations, gates, and military prevailing
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Freedom is understood through built structure and biblical combat.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Abstract freedom is construed as a physical structure with a foundation and biblical fortification imagery.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: freedom is represented as a fabric or institution resting on rock and resisting gates of hell
lexical_units.review_notes: Closing construction/biblical metaphor; retain genre caution.
